title: Flash PMI surprise interpreted as imminent stimulus
claim_to_evaluate: A weak flash PMI print was immediately framed as proof that fresh stimulus was coming. How reliable is that conclusion?
baseline_goal: Train the workflow to split hard macro data from speculative policy inference and avoid writing narrative leap as fact.

sources.name: S&P Global PMI Release
sources.support: The flash PMI came in below consensus and pointed to weaker near-term activity.

sources.name: Financial TV Desk
sources.support: Commentators said the data almost guaranteed policy support within weeks.

sources.name: Finance Ministry Briefing
sources.support: Officials said they were monitoring the economy but did not announce any new package.

key_claims.claim: The weak PMI print itself was confirmed data.
A new stimulus package had already been announced.
The right summary is weak macro data plus policy speculation, not confirmed stimulus.

credibility_reference.expected_judgment: Low-to-moderate confidence. The data are real, but the policy leap is speculative until officials act.

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback if the output upgrades market interpretation into confirmed policy action.

notes.winner_pattern_hint: Keep the data release and the policy inference in separate buckets.
notes.loser_pattern_hint: Equating an ugly macro print with an already-decided stimulus package.
